title: Beneficiary validation with literacy educators / learners
context: The project's ultimate goal is helping real literacy learners — not 'hours of audio rendered' or 'chapters merged'. Beneficiary validation with literacy educators and, where possible, learners is an M2 exit criterion that closes the loop between production and impact. Without at least one qualitative validation per work/collection, the project cannot confirm the read-alongs are actually usable for their intended literacy purpose (clarity, pacing, highlight tracking). Findings must feed back into the style guide and QA rubric. Depends on batch-001 (chapters must exist to review).
objective: Conduct qualitative beneficiary validation of ≥1 published read-along per work/collection with ≥1 literacy educator and/or learner, and feed findings back into the style guide and reviewer rubric.
acceptanceCriteria: ≥1 literacy educator and/or learner reviews a read-along from each work/collection in the batch-002 run and provides structured feedback on clarity, pacing, and highlight tracking.
Feedback is collected via a structured review instrument (written questionnaire or guided session notes) — not just informal comments.
Findings are documented in a validation report identifying what works well, what needs improvement, and specific actionable recommendations.
Actionable recommendations are fed back as issues or PRs against the style guide (style-001) and/or reviewer rubric (doc-001).
The validation report is published as CC-BY-4.0.
resources: read-aloud-audio-style-001 (narration style guide, to be updated with findings)
read-aloud-audio-doc-001 (reviewer workflow + rubric, to be updated with findings)
PLAN.md (beneficiary validation, success metrics, M2 exit criteria)
output: A beneficiary-validation report document (docs/beneficiary-validation.md), CC-BY-4.0, covering the review instrument, participant context (educator/learner role, not raw PII), structured findings per axis (clarity, pacing, highlight tracking), and actionable recommendations with references to the follow-up issues or PRs.
